title: A comparison of three stool tests for colorectal cancer screening.
journal_full: Medsurg nursing : official journal of the Academy of Medical-Surgical Nurses
abstract: The annual guaiac or immunochemical fecal occult blood test (FOBT) is one of the five colorectal cancer (CRC) screening regimens recommended by the American Cancer Society (Smith, Cokkinides, & Eyre, 2005). Stool-based deoxyribonucleic acid (DNA) testing for CRC is considered a promising technology (Smith, Cokkinides, & Eyre, 2003). Numerous features of three noninvasive stool tests for CRC are compared.
